title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.
Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Order the Production Defense RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Production Defense assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 8377 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Production Defense has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing unit tests and a local Docker demo proves production messaging readiness.
